rationale: In the citing paper, Libai, Muller, and Peres (2013) is used as part of the prior seeding literature that is interpreted as favoring high-status/influential targets. That prior position is central to the citing paper's setup of a 'traditional influencer' benchmark and motivates its alternative risk-versus-return view of seeding. The citing paper then presents empirical and simulation evidence that this benchmark can be ineffective in unpaid UGC networks, so the citation plays a meaningful contrastive role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper positions Libai, Muller, and Peres (2013) as representative of an influencer-focused seeding logic and then argues against that logic in its context. It shows that when response probability declines with status distance, low-status targeting can outperform high-status seeding for unknown creators.
